sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Brandon, is experiencing neck pain following a car crash in February of this year.
He reports that he was in multiple car crashes, including one in February, and has been experiencing neck pain since then.
He rates his pain as a 12 out of 10, describing it as sharp and incapacitating.
He also reports experiencing stiffness, headaches, dizziness, visual disturbances, numbness in his left arm and right leg, and muscle spasms.
He has been experiencing fatigue since the accident.
Past medical history: The patient has had multiple car crashes, including one in February of this year, and has undergone surgery after one of the crashes.
He has also been to therapy for several months after one of the crashes.
He was prescribed fentanyl by the pain clinic, but has not received a prescription for several weeks.
Review of systems: The patient reports experiencing stiffness, headaches, dizziness, visual disturbances, numbness in his left arm and right leg, and muscle spasms.
Current medications: Fentanyl (dose and frequency not specified) for pain.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient reports pain on palpation of the bony process and muscle of the neck.
He is unable to move his neck from side to side or bend it forward and backward due to pain.
There is no bruising, swelling, or laceration visible on the neck.
Test Results: Not mentioned.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a neck sprain, which is a diagnosis that can be explained by multiple factors, including the car crash in February.
Problem list: Neck sprain; Active.
Plan:
I prescribed Robaxin 1500mg every 6-8 hours for pain.
I also instructed the patient to apply ice to the affected area when experiencing bruising and swelling, and to perform exercises to maintain range of motion in the neck.
We will refer the patient to physical therapy after the MRI results are available.
If the patient's pain does not improve, we will consider local injections and refer him to pain medicine.
Follow-up: We will schedule a follow-up appointment after the MRI results are available to discuss further treatment options.
The patient will be given a note to provide to his employer excusing him from work until further notice.
